image_prompts.prompt: Hook visual: Your mental scratchpad is tiny — that's why phone numbers feel hard to keep.
Context visual: Working memory can only hold about 7 items at once, a limit set by cognitive psychologist George Miller.
Escalation visual: So, why do we struggle remembering longer numbers? It’s all about chunking.
Evidence visual: Chunking means grouping information into manageable bits, like 555-1234 instead of 5551234.
Pattern Interrupt visual: But did you know? Cowan suggests we can hold even fewer when distractions hit.
Payoff visual: Mastering chunking can boost your memory — whether for studying, phone numbers, or daily tasks.
Cta visual: Want to unlock more memory tips? Follow for more science hacks!
